belief_verification.belief: The current implementation does not yet provide a chain that commits proposed blocks.
belief_verification.evidence: No capability or workflow commits a proposed block; concept COMMIT resolves to 0 artifacts. A block-committed event exists but nothing emits it: blockchain::EV_BLOCK_COMMITTED_V0.

belief_verification.belief: A consensus loop already exists that proposes blocks and drives slot processing.
Adjacent subdomains exist: identity, wallet, transaction, mempool, consensus, orchestration.
belief_verification.evidence: identity via blockchain::ENTITY_ACTOR_V0; wallet via blockchain::ENTITY_WALLET_V0; transaction via blockchain::ENTITY_TRANSACTION_V0; mempool via blockchain::CC_WRITE_MEMPOOL_TX_V0; consensus via blockchain::WF_RUN_CONSENSUS_LOOP_V0; orchestration via blockchain::WF_RUN_CHAIN_SIMULATION_V0

entities.description: The authoritative, ordered, append-only ledger of committed blocks; the canonical record other subdomains rely on.
entities.store_model: An append-only record holding every committed block in order, plus a pointer to the most recent committed block.
entities.source_finding: S1 requested_outcomes #1; projection absent[COMMIT] — no ledger exists yet

entities.description: A unit of the ledger produced by a proposer; carries the transactions of its round and links to its predecessor once committed.
entities.store_model: Recorded as an element of the chain once committed; its content is hashed as its signature.

entities.description: The chain's first block; carries the first system transaction that mints the initial supply to the Genesis Actor.
entities.store_model: The single first element of the chain, created once at bootstrap.

entities.description: The permanent actor that receives the initial minted supply at bootstrap and owns the mint wallet thereafter.
entities.store_model: An actor record; the owning identity of the initial supply.
entities.source_finding: S1 business_vocabulary Genesis Actor; actor records exist (blockchain::ENTITY_ACTOR_V0) but no genesis actor is provisioned

entity_attributes.meaning: A hash of the block's content, computed at commit, that uniquely identifies the block.
A reference to the single block that precedes this one on the canonical chain (absent only for genesis).
The transactions recorded by the block in its round; they become authoritative on commit.
The most recently committed block, against which the next block's predecessor link is checked.
The first system transaction crediting the initial supply to the Genesis Actor.
entity_attributes.source_finding: S1 known_facts #3; minting exists (blockchain::WF_MINT_V0) but not as a genesis step

business_processes.process: Commit a proposed block to the chain
business_processes.initiator: The consensus loop that produced the proposed block
business_processes.outcome: The block is hashed, linked to its predecessor, and recorded as canonical; the block and its transactions become authoritative and immutable.

business_processes.initiator: The one-time system bootstrap, before the consensus loop runs
business_processes.outcome: A genesis block is created and the initial supply is minted to the Genesis Actor, establishing the chain and initial monetary state.

business_processes.initiator: The chain, after a block is committed
business_processes.outcome: Wallet balances are recomputed from the committed transactions and made consistent with the canonical committed history; balances are derived, not owned as independent state.
business_processes.source_finding: S1 known_facts #11; S1 business_events Balance Reconciled; a balance-reconciled event exists (blockchain::EV_BALANCE_RECONCILED_V0)

process_steps.process: Commit a proposed block to the chain
process_steps.action: Check the proposed block's predecessor link matches the current chain head.

process_steps.process: Commit a proposed block to the chain
process_steps.action: Hash the block's content to produce its canonical signature.

process_steps.process: Commit a proposed block to the chain
process_steps.action: Append the signed block to the canonical record and advance the chain head.
process_steps.record_produced: A new committed block on the ledger and a block-committed event.
process_steps.source_finding: S1 business_events Block Committed; event exists (blockchain::EV_BLOCK_COMMITTED_V0), no producer

process_steps.action: Create the genesis block containing the initial mint transaction.
Mint the fixed initial supply to the Genesis Actor and record it as the first committed block.
process_steps.record_produced: The initial supply credited to the Genesis Actor; the chain established at height one.
process_steps.source_finding: S1 known_facts #4; minting exists (blockchain::WF_MINT_V0) but not as genesis

process_steps.action: After a block commits, recompute each affected wallet balance from the committed transactions and record the reconciled balance.
process_steps.record_produced: Reconciled wallet balances and a balance-reconciled event.
process_steps.source_finding: S1 known_facts #11; a balance-reconciled event exists (blockchain::EV_BALANCE_RECONCILED_V0)

pps_baseline_fqdns.capability: Propose a block from the consensus loop
pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Selects a proposer, drains the mempool, and forms a proposed block in a round.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Does not commit the proposed block to a canonical ledger.

pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Defines the block record the chain commits.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Schema only; carries no commit or predecessor-link behaviour.

pps_baseline_fqdns.capability: Form a block from claimed mempool transactions
pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Assembles a proposed block from claimed transactions.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Produces a proposed block; does not record it as canonical.

pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Marks that a candidate block exists.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Observation only; not the commit trigger.

pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Declares that a block became canonical.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Declared but unemitted — no capability produces it today.

pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Mints value under a mint policy.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Not wired as a one-time genesis bootstrap step before the consensus loop.

pps_baseline_fqdns.what_it_does: Drives slot processing and block proposal.
pps_baseline_fqdns.cannot_do: Produces proposals; has no downstream commit-to-chain step.
Does not declare a chain/ledger store.

gaps.gap: No capability commits a proposed block to a canonical chain.
gaps.impact: The core requested outcome — an authoritative ledger of committed blocks — cannot be met; proposed blocks have nowhere to become canonical.

gaps.gap: No canonical chain store exists to hold committed blocks and track the chain head.
gaps.impact: There is no durable, append-only record for committed history.

gaps.gap: No genesis bootstrap capability creates the first block and mints the initial supply before consensus runs.
gaps.impact: The chain cannot be initialised with its fixed initial supply and Genesis Actor.

gaps.gap: No predecessor-link validation guards commit, so nothing enforces one predecessor per committed block.
gaps.impact: Without it the immutability and single-predecessor invariants cannot be upheld at commit.

architectural_observations.observation: The system already produces proposed blocks through a consensus loop but has no ledger to record them, so the chain plugs in directly downstream of proposal.
A block-committed event is already declared but nothing emits it, indicating a commit path was anticipated in the design and left unbuilt.
Minting already exists and can serve the genesis supply, so bootstrap reuses minting rather than introducing a new monetary mechanism.
Wallet balances are not chain-owned state; they are derived from committed transactions and reconciled on-chain after commit, and a balance-reconciled event already exists to carry that moment.
architectural_observations.source_finding: S1 known_facts #11; S1 authority_boundaries Wallet Balance

discovery_concerns.concern: The block-committed event exists with no producer, so its intended payload and emitter are unknown and must be settled when the commit path is designed.

open_questions.question: What fields must the block-committed event carry to be useful to downstream subdomains?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The event already exists but is unemitted; its payload shapes how other subdomains observe committed history.
